newsArray.description: Cricket broadcasting: ಭಾರತೀಯ ವೀಕ್ಷಕರ ಅನುಕೂಲಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ಇಸಿಬಿ ಈ ನಿರ್ಧಾರ ಮಾಡಿದ್ದು, ಸರಣಿಯು ಸೋನಿ ಸ್ಪೋರ್ಟ್ಸ್ ನೆಟ್ವರ್ಕ್ನಲ್ಲಿ ಪ್ರಸಾರವಾಗಲಿದೆ. ಏಕದಿನ ಸರಣಿಯ ಎಲ್ಲ ಟಿಕೆಟ್ಗಳು ಈಗಾಗಲೇ ಸಂಪೂರ್ಣವಾಗಿ ಮಾರಾಟವಾಗಿವೆ.
creator: ಪಿಟಿಐ

newsArray.title: Who Is Manav Suthar? Indian Left Arm Spinner Shines On Dream Test Debut Vs Afghanistan
newsArray.description: Manav Suthar has emerged as India’s newest spin sensation after a dream Test debut against Afghanistan, taking a six-wicket haul. The Rajasthan left-arm spinner has quickly become a talking point in Indian cricket. From domestic grind to a six-wicket Test debut — Manav Suthar’s rise is exactly the kind of breakout story that defines India’s next spin generation.
creator: omair pall

newsArray.title: India envoy sees Norway Chess as catalyst for community engagement in Oslo
newsArray.description: Oslo, Jun 8 (PTI) With Norway Chess moving from Stavanger to Oslo, Indian Ambassador to Norway, Gloria Gangte, expressed hope that the tournament's relocation will strengthen the chess ecosystem in the Norwegian capital and inspire aspiring Indian players living there to take up the sport more seriously and pursue it professionally. Norway Chess, which was launched in Stavanger in 2013, was staged in Oslo this year and proved especially fruitful for India. R. Praggnanandhaa became the first Indian to win the prestigious six-player tournament and also defeated world No. 1 Magnus Carlsen twice during the event -- a feat achieved by only a select few players worldwide. The tournament's move to Oslo has also sparked optimism about its potential to grow the game among the Indian community in Norway. Gangte said the shift from Stavanger to the capital could help raise awareness about the tournament and create opportunities for greater engagement with young players. "That's something we can explore with Norway Chess in terms of collaboration. Oslo is a much bigger city than Stavanger and has a larger Indian population, particularly students and families who move here for educational opportunities. Having Norway Chess based in Oslo makes it more visible and accessible to a wider audience," Gangte said. She added that the embassy could potentially work with organisers on initiatives such as workshops and outreach programmes for children to encourage greater participation in the sport. "Let's see how it develops. The organisers, I'm sure they will find ways to build on this opportunity," she said. With India set to host the 2030 Commonwealth Games and pursuing a bid for the 2036 Olympic Games, Gangte said there is scope for greater sporting collaboration between the two countries, particularly given Norway's expertise in technology and sports science. Asked whether India and Norway could explore a sports partnership, Gangte noted that sport is an area that has largely remained untapped in bilateral relations but suggested that could change. "Every time we speak of India-Norway relations, we usually talk about the blue economy, the green transition, maritime affairs, digitisation, as well as polar and Arctic research, and all of that. "But sports is one area which has really not been taken up. So, I think we must now look at the scope, and if there is any interest from our side, let's say from our sports community or the Norwegian, you know, sports institutions, we would be very happy in having that collaboration developed. "Frankly, now I would say that it's almost non-existent, but at an individual level like this, like Norway Chess is doing, or there are some football players, there are some cricket players, you know, there are, so there are these individual players who are really getting connections, but not at an institutional level of cooperation," she said. Asked about her impressions of Praggnanandhaa, who won the Norway Chess title, and Divya Deshmukh, she said both had come across as exceptionally humble despite their achievements. "I think they are very humble people, despite what they have achieved. And I think that grounding is something that people need to have when they are successful. And also, the resilience," she said. PTI AM AM AH AH
creator: pti
